intro: Python and TypeScript share a commitment to developer productivity, but their type systems differ fundamentally — Python's type hints are optional and runtime-ignored, while TypeScript enforces structural typing at compile time. When converting, Python's `dict`, `list`, and `Optional` type hints map to TypeScript interfaces, generics, and union types. Python dataclasses translate naturally to TypeScript interfaces or classes with typed properties.

diffs.from: Type hints (PEP 484) are optional and ignored at runtime.
diffs.to: Types are enforced at compile time by tsc. Violations are build errors.

diffs.from: Protocols define structural interfaces (PEP 544). Abstract base classes too.
diffs.to: interface and type alias define structural contracts enforced by the compiler.

diffs.from: Optional[X] = Union[X, None]. None is a valid value of any type by default.
diffs.to: X | null or X | undefined. Strict mode requires explicit null narrowing.

diffs.from: TypeVar and Generic[T] for parameterised classes. Runtime erasure.
diffs.to: Generic syntax <T> on functions, classes, and interfaces. Compile-time only.

diffs.from: Interpreted directly by CPython. No build step.
diffs.to: Transpiled to JavaScript by tsc or bundlers like esbuild/Vite/SWC.

examples.title: Typed function with Optional argument
examples.description: Python Optional[str] maps to TypeScript string | null. Default values and return types translate directly.
examples.fromCode:
from typing import Optional

def greet(name: str, prefix: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
    if prefix is None:
        return "Hello, " + name
    return prefix + " " + name

print(greet("Alice"))          # Hello, Alice
print(greet("Bob", "Dr."))     # Dr. Bob

examples.title: Typed dataclass → TypeScript interface + class
examples.description: Python dataclasses map cleanly to TypeScript interfaces for the shape and classes for behaviour.

tips.title: Optional[X] → X | null
tips.body: Python Optional[X] means X | None. In TypeScript strict mode write X | null. Enable strictNullChecks to get compile-time null safety equivalent to Python mypy.

tips.title: dict → interface or Record<K,V>
tips.body: Python dict[str, int] maps to Record<string, number>. If keys are known, define a TypeScript interface instead for better IDE autocompletion.

tips.title: List[X] → X[] or Array<X>
tips.body: Python List[int] becomes number[] or Array<number>. Tuple[int, str] becomes [number, string] — a tuple type in TypeScript.

tips.title: Dataclass → interface + class
tips.body: Simple dataclasses with only data become TypeScript interfaces. Add a class wrapper only if you need methods or a constructor with default logic.
